decisions.question: Address issue 183 with server-side contentHash, client skip-on-hash, and bounded parallel bootstrap reads
decisions.chosen: Address issue 183 with server-side contentHash, client skip-on-hash, and bounded parallel bootstrap reads
decisions.reasoning: The current checkout already uses in-process HTTP/2-capable Go clients and has export support, so the remaining hot path is sequential per-entry ReadFile during tree fallback and the lack of local hash reuse when state is wiped.

decisions.question: Address issue 183 with server-side contentHash, client skip-on-hash, and bounded parallel bootstrap reads
decisions.chosen: Address issue 183 with server-side contentHash, client skip-on-hash, and bounded parallel bootstrap reads
decisions.reasoning: The current checkout already uses in-process HTTP/2-capable Go clients and has export support, so the remaining hot path is sequential per-entry ReadFile during tree fallback and the lack of local hash reuse when state is wiped.

decisions.question: Found http.Client.Timeout as 6th defect via live testing; removed whole-request cap in favor of granular transport + context cancellation
decisions.chosen: Found http.Client.Timeout as 6th defect via live testing; removed whole-request cap in favor of granular transport + context cancellation
decisions.reasoning: context decoupling alone was insufficient; net/http enforces Client.Timeout over body read independent of ctx

decisions.question: Review found Notion writeback discovery/layout mismatch
decisions.chosen: Review found Notion writeback discovery/layout mismatch
decisions.reasoning: Notion layout advertises pages/comments/databases writeback resources, but generated discovery/resources only cover database page create; verify script iterates discovery-data endpoints and does not assert layout writeback resources have sibling schemas.

decisions.question: Build issue #182 on top of issue #180 lifecycle branch
decisions.chosen: Build issue #182 on top of issue #180 lifecycle branch
decisions.reasoning: The current workspace is already on codex/issue-180-mount-daemon-lifecycle, and #182 is explicitly triggered by the double-daemon state race fixed there; stacking preserves that dependency while leaving unrelated backup files untouched.

decisions.question: Require explicit local mutation state for writeback retries
decisions.chosen: Require explicit local mutation state for writeback retries
decisions.reasoning: Issue #182 shows hash drift alone can be caused by remote hydration or corrupted state; writes should be retried from Dirty state set by watcher/local-create paths, and deletes from a separate deletePending flag set by local delete handling.

decisions.question: Harden process scan feedback cases
decisions.reasoning: PR feedback showed legacy bare pid files needed live process corroboration, relayfile start is a mount alias, and local-dir matching needed path-token boundaries instead of substring matching.

decisions.question: Retry cloud refresh with freshly reloaded disk credentials after refresh-token auth rejection
decisions.chosen: Retry cloud refresh with freshly reloaded disk credentials after refresh-token auth rejection
decisions.reasoning: Concurrent CLI processes can rotate cloud-credentials.json between the daemon's load and refresh POST; a one-shot reload absorbs that race before entering degraded re-login state.

decisions.question: Use process-scan fallback for mount daemon lifecycle
decisions.chosen: Use process-scan fallback for mount daemon lifecycle
decisions.reasoning: Issue 180 reports stale pid files and reparented child binaries; scanning live relayfile mount commands lets stop/status/start handle orphan daemons even when .relay/mount.pid is wrong.

decisions.question: Used fetch and non-destructive pull checks because relayfile and cloud have local/conflicted work
decisions.chosen: Used fetch and non-destructive pull checks because relayfile and cloud have local/conflicted work
decisions.reasoning: relayfile has unresolved merge conflicts and cloud has dirty files that Git reported would be overwritten; preserving user/agent work is higher priority while still refreshing remote refs

decisions.question: Close PR39 gaps in isolated worktrees
decisions.chosen: Close PR39 gaps in isolated worktrees
decisions.reasoning: Latest origin/main showed skills PR39 files missing from skills, Python SDK and mounted skill text still referenced the retired provider .layout.md path, cloud lacked hosted-sync by-edited regression coverage, and relayfile-adapters documented the digest/layout contract without pinning the activity-summary by-edited fallback.

decisions.question: Explain lagging providers with zero measured lag in relayfile status
decisions.chosen: Explain lagging providers with zero measured lag in relayfile status
decisions.reasoning: The server can report status=lagging with lagSeconds=0 when a provider has no cursor or watermark; querying ingress lets the CLI show whether events are pending, accepted, or absent.

keyFindings: "implement-slice" completed → >7u•Booting MCP server: codex_apps(0s • esc to interrupt)›Summarize recent commitsgpt-5.5 high · Context 100% left · ~/P
"fix-loop" completed → No commits, pushes, PR opens, source edits, or destructive git operations were performed.

"final-review-claude" completed → ## Summary

Re-reviewed the fixed child state of slice `update-last-week` from scratch, independently re-running every v
"lead-plan" completed → File ends with `RICKY_MASTER_LEAD_PLAN_READY` as required. No source files were edited.
narrative: This work session cluster spans a week of relayfile runtime hardening and workspace-primitive contract closure. The most impactful chain was issues #180→#182→#183: issue #180 hardened the mount daemon lifecycle with process-scan fallback discovery for orphan daemons (stale pid files, reparented child binaries); issue #182 stacked on top to add writeback guards requiring explicit Dirty/deletePending local mutation state before retrying writes (preventing hash-drift from remote hydration from triggering false writebacks); issue #183 then optimized bootstrap performance by adding decoded-byte contentHash to tree/events endpoints, skipping ReadFile calls when local bytes match, and fetching remaining files through a bounded concurrent worker pool. A separate fix addressed cloud refresh-token rotation races with a one-shot disk-credential reload/retry before entering degraded re-login state. In parallel, a large multi-agent workflow (ricky-workspace-primitive-skills-pr-39) drove contract closure across four repos (relayfile, cloud, relayfile-adapters, skills). Key canonical decisions were locked: `<provider>/LAYOUT.md` over `.layout.md`, existing YAML frontmatter (`date`, `generated_at`, `covers`) over the skill's blockquote header, sibling `.schema.json` as the writeback discovery surface, and `writeback retry` as the supported replay path. The workflow patched missing skill manifest files, fixed Python SDK and mounted skill text still referencing the retired `.layout.md` path, added hosted by-edited regression coverage in cloud, and updated adapter fallback contract docs. A Codex validator agent independently discovered that `http.Client.Timeout` enforces a whole-request cap over body reads independent of context cancellation, leading to its removal in favor of granular transport timeouts. Two diagnostic sessions investigated confusing `status=lagging` with `lagSeconds=0` for providers lacking cursor/watermark progress. The root cause was absent ingress state rather than timed backlog. A PR (#153) added CLI diagnostics querying sync ingress to render the actual reason.

decisions.question: How should writeback retries be gated to prevent false writebacks from hash drift?
decisions.chosen: Require explicit Dirty state (set by watcher/local-create) for write retries and a separate deletePending flag for delete retries
decisions.reasoning: Issue #182 showed hash drift can be caused by remote hydration or corrupted state, not just local mutations. Using hash difference alone as a retry signal is unsafe.
decisions.impact: internal/mountsync/syncer.go now gates writeback paths on explicit local mutation flags, preventing remote-only changes from being echoed back as writes

decisions.question: How to handle stale pid files and orphan mount daemons?
decisions.chosen: Process-scan fallback: scan live relayfile mount commands to discover orphan daemons even when .relay/mount.pid is wrong
decisions.reasoning: Issue #180 reports stale pid files and reparented child binaries; pid file alone is unreliable for lifecycle management
decisions.impact: Stop/status/start commands now work correctly even with corrupted pid state; PR feedback further required live process corroboration for legacy bare pid files and path-token boundary matching

decisions.question: How to optimize bootstrap performance for large workspaces?
decisions.chosen: Server-side contentHash on tree/events, client skip-on-hash, and bounded parallel bootstrap reads
decisions.reasoning: The hot path was sequential per-entry ReadFile during tree fallback plus no local hash reuse when state is wiped; HTTP/2 clients and export support already existed
decisions.impact: Bootstrap skips files whose local bytes already match the tree hash; remaining files are fetched through a bounded concurrent worker pool instead of sequentially

decisions.question: How to handle concurrent CLI processes rotating cloud refresh tokens?
decisions.chosen: One-shot reload of disk credentials and retry after refresh-token auth rejection, before entering degraded re-login state
decisions.reasoning: Concurrent CLI processes can rotate cloud-credentials.json between the daemon's load and refresh POST
decisions.impact: internal/relayfile/store.go absorbs the rotation race without forcing user re-authentication

decisions.question: What is the canonical provider layout path?
decisions.chosen: `<provider>/LAYOUT.md` — update skill contracts to match runtime, do not migrate runtime to `.layout.md`
decisions.reasoning: Cloud and adapters already emit `LAYOUT.md`; migrating runtime would be higher churn than updating the skill spec
decisions.impact: Python SDK and mounted skill text referencing `.layout.md` were patched across repos

decisions.question: Should http.Client.Timeout be used for large-workspace mount convergence?
decisions.chosen: Remove whole-request http.Client.Timeout in favor of granular transport timeouts plus context cancellation
decisions.reasoning: net/http enforces Client.Timeout over body read independent of ctx; context decoupling alone was insufficient for long-running bootstrap streams
decisions.impact: Large workspace bootstrap no longer times out mid-stream during slow body reads

decisions.question: What writeback states should be exposed to agents?
decisions.chosen: Expose only `pending` and `dead` via `writeback list`; remove `succeeded`/`failed` from the contract
decisions.reasoning: Current local list only returns per-op rows for `dead`; other states error because only aggregate counters are tracked. Long-form history belongs in metrics/logs.
decisions.impact: Simplifies agent-facing writeback monitoring contract and aligns CLI with actual backend capabilities

conventions.pattern: Stack dependent issue branches: #182 built on #180's `codex/issue-180-mount-daemon-lifecycle` branch
conventions.rationale: Issues triggered by the same root cause (double-daemon state race) share implementation context; stacking preserves dependency while keeping unrelated backup files untouched
conventions.scope: Branch management for causally related issues

conventions.pattern: Run `scripts/check-contract-surface.sh` after any handler or OpenAPI changes
Use repo-specific worktrees for cross-repo contract changes; land adapters first, then cloud, then relayfile
conventions.rationale: Each repo has its own tests, release cadence, and local dirty state; dependency order prevents consuming contracts before they exist

conventions.pattern: Digest file updates must emit normal file events but `/digests/*` writes must not trigger digest regeneration
conventions.rationale: Mounted workspaces need to receive changed digest files, but recursion would cause infinite regeneration loops
conventions.scope: Any code path that writes to /digests/

conventions.pattern: Preserve terminal provider state (closed, merged, archived) as data, not file deletes
conventions.rationale: Upstream object not being deleted means the record should remain readable; agents need historical state

lessons.lesson: Hash drift alone is not a reliable signal for local mutation — remote hydration and corrupted state can cause identical symptoms
lessons.context: Issue #182 writeback guard: the syncer was retrying writes based solely on hash mismatch, which could be triggered by remote-side changes
lessons.recommendation: Always gate writeback retries on explicit local mutation state (Dirty flag from watcher, deletePending from local delete handler) rather than inferring mutation from content differences

lessons.lesson: net/http Client.Timeout enforces a hard deadline over the entire request including body read, independent of context cancellation
lessons.context: Codex validator agent discovered this as a 6th defect during live testing of large-workspace mount convergence; bootstrap streams were being killed mid-read
lessons.recommendation: For streaming or long-running HTTP body reads, use granular transport-level timeouts (TLSHandshakeTimeout, ResponseHeaderTimeout) plus per-operation context deadlines instead of Client.Timeout

lessons.lesson: Provider status=lagging with lagSeconds=0 means absent cursor/watermark, not zero backlog
lessons.context: Diagnostic investigation found 6 providers reported as lagging with no measured lag, no errors, no dead letters — only Linear had ingress/cursor progress
lessons.recommendation: When displaying ambiguous server state, query underlying signals (ingress events, cursor presence) and render the actual reason rather than passing through the bare status code

lessons.lesson: Multi-agent review workflows surface real defects but produce high coordination overhead
lessons.context: The ricky-child-update-last-week-workflow took 71 hours wall-clock with 7 agents cycling through plan→implement→review→fix→re-review→codex-review→codex-fix; the initial review verdict was BLOCKED
lessons.recommendation: For bounded implementation slices, a single review+fix pass is usually sufficient; reserve multi-pass review cycles for security-critical or cross-repo contract changes

lessons.lesson: Fetching remote refs non-destructively is safer than pulling when repos have local conflicts or dirty files
lessons.context: relayfile had unresolved merge conflicts and cloud had dirty files that git pull would overwrite; the workflow used fetch + inspection instead
lessons.recommendation: When coordinating across repos with potentially dirty state, use `git fetch` and inspect `origin/main` rather than pulling, to preserve in-progress agent or user work

openQuestions: Issues #180/#182/#183 have uncommitted working-tree changes (syncer.go, store.go, syncer_test.go, etc.) on codex/issue-182-writeback-guard — these need to be committed and PR'd or the work will be lost
The 500-event digest cap in cloud has not been resolved — needs either removal, pagination/chunking, or explicit machine-readable warning semantics
by-edited/<date>/ fallback indexes are still unimplemented across all three repos despite being required by the activity-summary skill contract
Workspace timezone support for digest window boundaries remains UTC-only; no wall-clock scheduler exists for daily/weekly closing windows
The websocket.go change in the working tree (contentHash forwarding for issue #183) needs review to ensure it doesn't break existing event consumers
Notion writeback discovery/layout mismatch found during PR39 review — layout advertises pages/comments/databases writeback but discovery only covers database page create
